item.text: The finding points at the VAU HTTP client’s signed public key trust validation. I’m reading those classes and nearby tests/usages now to identify the intended verification path and where certificate validation is being bypassed.

The core bug is in the signature verification result: `Signature.verify(...)` is called, but its boolean return value is ignored, so a bad signature still reaches `return true`. I’m checking test structure next, then I’ll add a focused regression test around that validator.

item.text: I’m going to make the production change by treating a `false` ECDSA verification result as trust failure. The regression test will drive the full validator path with fetched certificate data and a deliberately invalid ES256 signature, while keeping certificate-chain validation mocked so the test stays isolated to this bug.

item.text: I found the expected signing format in `SignedPublicVauKeys`: signatures are raw ES256/P1363 bytes that are transcoded to DER before verification. I’m adding a test that supplies a structurally valid but wrong raw signature, which would have been accepted before because the false result was discarded.
The fix and regression test are in place. I’m running the VAU HTTP client test suite now; if formatting or provider assumptions show up, I’ll adjust locally.

item.text:
Implemented the remediation.

Changed [SignedPublicKeysTrustValidatorImpl.java](/work/vau/vau-httpclient/src/main/java/com/oviva/telematik/vau/httpclient/internal/SignedPublicKeysTrustValidatorImpl.java:44) so ECDSA verification must return `true`; a `false` result now fails trust validation instead of falling through to `return true`.

Added a regression test in [SignedPublicKeysTrustValidatorImplTest.java](/work/vau/vau-httpclient/src/test/java/com/oviva/telematik/vau/httpclient/internal/SignedPublicKeysTrustValidatorImplTest.java:29) that exercises the validator with certificate data and an invalid ES256 signature, expecting `isTrusted(...)` to return `false`.

Verification: attempted `mvn -pl vau/vau-httpclient test`, but Maven could not resolve parent BOMs from Maven Central because `repo.maven.apache.org` failed DNS resolution in this environment. Offline mode also failed because those artifacts are not cached locally.
